articles.title: US capture of Iranian cargo ship is escalation and retaliation, experts say

children.content: Donald Trump says the Iranian-flagged ship ignored warnings from the US Navy, but Iran says the action is piracy and breaches the ceasefire terms.

image.altText: A view of Iranian-flagged cargo ship M/V Touska from the deck of a us navy vessel

children.content: Iran is yet to formally outline whether it has decided to send a negotiating team, with Tehran stating many of the US's positions are "unrealistic".

items.title: Why Australia's big gas producers haven't jacked up domestic prices despite war

children.content: After a decade and a half of mostly failed threats from three prime ministers and constant surveillance by the competition regulator, the east coast gas export giants are suddenly putting Australians first.

image.altText: A huge pill-shaped tank with giant red letters LNG on a cargo ship.

items.title: Gas tax fight escalates ahead of budget as inquiry begins

children.content: Calls are growing for a new 25 per cent tax on gas exports to increase the petroleum resource rent tax on windfall profits, but companies have warned any changes would jeopardise Australia's energy supply.

image.altText: David Pocock at a press conference at Parliament House

items.title: Tsunami warning as magnitude-7.7 earthquake hits north-east Japan

children.content: An earthquake with a magnitude of 7.7 struck off the north-eastern coast of Japan, triggering tsunami warnings and widespread evacuation orders affecting around 170,000 people.

image.altText: NHK news report of Japan earthquake and tsunami on April 20th, 2026

items.title: Did you really get a discount on your Tim Tams? The ACCC doesn't think so

children.content: The sequel to Australia’s supermarket pricing wars is about to kick off in the federal court as the ACCC takes on Woolworths over what it argues are misleading pricing practices.

image.altText: Close up of the Woolworths logo and a special sign in the background.

items.title: More Australian women than men are now getting ADHD medication

children.content: Within the sharp increase of ADHD rates in Australia lies a hidden story — more women are being prescribed medication than men. Understanding why is more complicated.

gallery.altText: A graphic image with silhouettes of men and women's side profiles overlaid with medication and two upwards arrows.

items.title: UK PM shifts blame to Foreign Office in bid to contain Mandelson scandal

image.altText: Sir Keir Starmer walking out of Downing Street holding a folder.

items.title: NRL overhauls training rules in landmark player safety move

children.content: The move to restrict training loads is aimed at mitigating injuries, including player head injuries, which continue to be a major issue for all contact sports.

items.title: FBI director sues The Atlantic for $348m over report detailing misconduct

children.content: Kash Patel denies all allegations in the report, which quotes numerous sources alleging drinking and absences.

items.title: Bruce the kea rules the roost using his broken beak like a sword

children.content: Without a curvy upper beak to get in the way, Bruce the kea uses his lower beak to stab or joust at other birds in his social group.

gallery.altText: A portrait of a green parrot with its head looking side-on, the upper beak is missing from below the nostrils.

items.title: US singer D4vd charged with murdering 14yo girl found in his Tesla

image.altText: David Burke sitting on a couch wearing shorts, a yellow mesh top and sunglasses.

children.content: The 21-year-old, who rose to fame after going viral on TikTok, is facing charges including first-degree murder, lewd and lascivious acts with an individual under 14, and mutilating a body.

items.title: Fair Work Commission makes 'historic' fuel order for truck drivers

children.content: The Fair Work Commission orders that retailers, miners and other companies that use truck drivers and transport operators must hold twice-monthly reviews of fuel prices and adjust their rates accordingly.

image.altText: A large silver truck drives off into a pink sunset.

items.title: EV drivers are being left waiting with just 45 chargers per car in Australia

children.content: With no end in sight to soaring fuel prices, 7.30 decided to take an EV for a test drive to see how far it could take our reporter, and if Australia's EV charging network stacks up.

image.altText: Cars parked at an EV charging station.

items.title: How to keep your dog happy living in an apartment

children.content: Providing opportunities for your dog to explore, giving them a safe and comfortable space to retreat to — as well as a good routine — are key to keeping your dog happy.

items.title: AFL apologises after Butters appeal upheld, umpire abuse conviction quashed

children.content: The AFL appeals board has quashed Zak Butters's conviction for abusive language against umpire Nick Foot, saying a Tribunal member listening in while driving his car for part of the original hearing constitutes a miscarriage of justice.

items.title: 'I had no-one to talk to': The 92yo trying to save his dying language
